hero.badge: Sci-Fi Action Adventure with a Hacking Twist
hero.title: PRAGMATA Wiki
hero.description: Follow Hugh and Diana through a lunar research station with hack-and-shoot combat, Shelter upgrades, story guides, demo help, and platform info.

gameFeature.title: Pragmata Gameplay Overview
gameFeature.description: Follow Hugh and Diana through a hostile lunar research station. Blend third-person shooting with live grid-based hacking to defeat armored bots and uncover the mystery of Lunafilament.

pragmataReleaseDate.title: Pragmata Release Date
pragmataReleaseDate.intro: Pragmata has had one of Capcom's longest modern release roads. The current headline date is April 17, 2026 on Capcom's official site and PlayStation, while Steam shows April 16, 2026 on its store page.

items.title: First reveal with a 2022 target
items.description: Pragmata debuted as a new Capcom science-fiction action-adventure and was originally positioned for a 2022 release window.

items.title: Window moved to 2023
items.description: Capcom later shifted the game out of 2022 and re-framed the launch window around 2023.

items.title: Launch timing pulled back again
items.description: A new teaser reintroduced the project but removed a near-term launch target, extending the wait beyond the earlier 2023 expectation.

items.title: Release moved forward by one week
items.description: Capcom announced the game would now arrive earlier, changing the date from April 24, 2026 to April 17, 2026.

items.title: What players see right now
items.description: Capcom's official site and PlayStation pages use April 17, 2026. Steam lists April 16, 2026. Both dates point to the same global release window.

pragmataGameplay.title: How Pragmata Gameplay Works
pragmataGameplay.intro: Pragmata blends third-person shooting, live puzzle-hacking, traversal, and environmental problem-solving into one loop. Hugh and Diana are always stronger together than apart.

steps.title: Move as Hugh, manage space before you commit
steps.description: Hugh handles aiming, firearms, dodging, and suit-based mobility. His thrusters also matter outside combat, helping you zip through the lunar facility and reach new paths.
highlights: Third-person combat built around constant movement
Suit thrusters support traversal and repositioning
Both combat and exploration begin from Hugh's movement

steps.title: Hack enemies with Diana to break their defenses
steps.description: Most enemy bots are armored, so raw gunfire is not enough by itself. Diana brings up a grid-style hacking panel that lets you weaken or open enemy defenses in real time.
highlights: Connecting more blue squares increases hacking effect
Successful hacks disable armor and expose weak points
Known node examples include Decode and Multi-Hack

steps.title: Convert the hack into damage with the right weapon
steps.description: Once enemies are opened up, Hugh cashes in with firearms. Some shields must be shot first, some missiles can be hacked back, and your weapon choice changes the pace of each fight.
highlights: Primary, Attack, Defense, and Tactical weapon roles
Red barrier enemies must be opened by gunfire first
Incoming missiles can be redirected with a successful hack

steps.title: Use station hacks, upgrades, and Shelter downtime to build your style
steps.description: Diana can unlock doors and clear blocked routes, while upgrade stations and the Shelter let players improve Hugh's gear, expand hacking options, and shape their preferred playstyle.
highlights: Diana hacks security systems to open progression routes
Upgrade stations improve weapons, stats, and hacking tools
Shelter acts as a recovery and progression hub between pushes

pragmataDemoGuide.title: Pragmata Sketchbook Demo Guide
pragmataDemoGuide.intro: Sketchbook is the best live entry point for players who want to understand Pragmata before launch. It is built to teach the game's two-track combat loop and the feel of moving through the lunar research station.

cards.name: Where to download it
cards.description: Sketchbook has dedicated store listings on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, and it is also surfaced from the main Steam product page.
bullets: Steam: listed from the main PRAGMATA store page

cards.name: What the demo includes
cards.description: Capcom describes Sketchbook as a hands-on slice of the combat system and lunar-station traversal. It shows how the game asks both halves of the duo to operate together.
bullets: Traversal through the lunar research station
A practical first look at the game's pace and control rhythm

cards.name: Platform and language details
cards.description: The console store pages confirm that the demo supports English and Japanese voice. PS5 and Xbox demo releases were listed as February 5, 2026.
bullets: PS5 and Xbox demo release: February 5, 2026
Xbox page highlights 4K Ultra HD, HDR10, 60fps+, and Dolby Atmos

cards.name: What players can learn before launch
cards.description: Sketchbook answers the most important pre-release questions better than trailers do. It lets players test whether the hacking layer feels satisfying under pressure.
bullets: Whether grid-based hacking stays readable during live fights
Whether Pragmata's tone, movement, and combat style match your taste

pragmataStoryExplained.title: Pragmata Story Explained
pragmataStoryExplained.intro: Pragmata's story begins with a lost lunar station, a reality-shaping material called Lunafilament, and an unlikely partnership between Hugh and Diana. The setup is easy to read at a high level, but it leaves plenty of room for mystery.

items.question: What is the basic setup of Pragmata?
items.answer: In the near future, humanity develops Lunafilament, a material that can create almost anything as long as the right data exists. When contact is lost with the lunar station researching it, a response team is sent in, and a massive lunar quake follows soon after their arrival.

items.question: Who are the two main characters in Pragmata?
items.answer: Hugh Williams is a member of the dispatch team sent to investigate the lunar facility. Diana is a Pragmata — a unique android created using Lunafilament — and she becomes Hugh's partner as the two try to survive the station and find a route back to Earth.

items.question: Why is Diana so important to Pragmata's story and gameplay?
items.answer: Diana is tied directly to the station's systems and to the game's central combat idea. She can hack enemy armor to reveal weak points, hack the station itself to open routes forward, and acts as the emotional center of the Hugh-Diana relationship.

items.question: What is the main conflict in Pragmata?
items.answer: Hugh and Diana are trapped inside a hostile lunar research station filled with enemy bots and shadowed by the mystery of the rogue AI IDUS. Their immediate goal is escape, but the larger tension comes from uncovering what went wrong and how the station's systems turned against them.

items.question: What are the Shelter and Cabin in Pragmata?
items.answer: Shelter is the duo's safe space away from the action, a place that can continue to grow over the course of the game. Cabin is the helpful bot assistant inside that space, giving the story a small island of warmth inside an otherwise cold and dangerous setting.

pragmataCharacters.title: Pragmata Characters
pragmataCharacters.intro: Hugh Williams and Diana are the core of Pragmata's story, combat loop, and exploration flow. Hugh is the human survivor trying to return to Earth after a lunar quake separates his team, while Diana is a Lunafilament-based android whose hacking abilities make survival possible inside the lunar research station.

items.title: Diana
items.description: Also known as D-I-0336-7, Diana is a Pragmata created using Lunafilament. She hacks enemy armor to expose weak points and can hack the lunar station itself to open blocked routes.
highlights: Unique android created using Lunafilament
Can hack enemy bots and the facility itself
Curious, quick to learn, and deeply attached to the name Hugh gives her

items.title: Hugh Williams
items.description: Hugh is a member of the dispatch team sent to investigate the lunar facility. Skilled with a wide range of firearms, he also uses his suit's thrusters to move through the station.
highlights: Separated from his team after a massive lunar quake
Proficient with multiple firearms and suit-based mobility
Blunt and sarcastic on the surface, but genuinely kind-hearted

items.title: Why Their Duo Matters
items.description: Pragmata's two-protagonist setup is not cosmetic. Diana creates openings by hacking armor, systems, and doors, while Hugh turns those openings into progress with guns, mobility, and field survival.
highlights: Diana controls access and enemy vulnerability
Hugh supplies damage, movement, and frontline action
Both characters are required for combat, traversal, and narrative progression

pragmataCombatAndHacking.title: Pragmata Combat and Hacking
pragmataCombatAndHacking.intro: Regular gunfire is not enough against armored bots, so every major encounter starts with hacking. Hugh locks onto a target, Diana brings up a grid-style Hacking Panel, and the player draws a continuous path to trigger the hack. Weapon choice, shield breaking, Hacking Nodes, and Shelter upgrades turn each fight into a rhythm of setup, exposure, and payoff.

steps.title: Lock on and trigger Diana's Hacking Panel
steps.description: When Hugh locks onto an enemy, Diana opens a grid-based panel. Drawing one continuous line through blue squares completes the hack.
highlights: A successful hack deals damage and temporarily compromises enemy armor
Longer paths increase damage and keep armor disabled longer
Grid connects in sequence — plan your path before committing

steps.title: Punish exposed weak points with Hugh's weapons
steps.description: Once Diana exposes the target, Hugh switches from setup to damage. The combat loop rewards turning a clean hack into immediate firearm pressure.
highlights: Hugh uses multiple gun types suited to different scenarios
The Primary Unit is the constant fallback weapon
Tactical and Attack options are meant for combo play and burst finishes

steps.title: Read enemy counterplay before committing
steps.description: Hacking is never free. Some enemies pressure you while the panel is open, forcing you to choose between a better hack or a quick dodge.
highlights: Red shield barriers block Diana's hacking entirely
Those shields must be broken first with Hugh's gun
Some homing missiles can be hacked and redirected back at enemies

steps.title: Use specialized weapons for tactical control
steps.description: Different weapons support different phases of the encounter and make the hack window safer or more rewarding.
highlights: Tactical Unit can pin enemies down during setup
Attack Unit excels at close-range finishing damage
Defense Unit can lure enemies away and create breathing room

steps.title: Boost hacks with Hacking Nodes
steps.description: If Diana is carrying a Hacking Node, yellow tiles appear on the panel. Connecting them activates extra effects and adds more tactical options to each successful hack.
highlights: Decode lowers enemy defense for easier follow-up
Multi-Hack spreads the effect to nearby targets simultaneously
Each activation consumes one Hacking Node

steps.title: Return to the Shelter to upgrade the loop
steps.description: The Shelter is the recovery and upgrade hub. It lets you heal, spend gathered Lunafilament, and improve both combat tools and hacking efficiency.
highlights: Upgrade weapons and expand your Hacking Node options
Carry more nodes for greater flexibility in fights
Unlock abilities like Critical Down for knockdown follow-ups

pragmataPlatformsAndEditions.title: Pragmata Platforms and Editions
pragmataPlatformsAndEditions.intro: If you are deciding where to buy Pragmata, the key questions are which platform you play on, what features the storefront version supports, and whether you want the extra Shelter Variety Pack. Standard pricing is set at 59.99 USD, while Deluxe is 69.99 USD.

highlights: PS5 Pro Enhanced
DualSense vibration and trigger effect supported
Remote Play and Game Help supported
4K Ultra HD, HDR10, Ray Tracing, 60 fps+
Spatial Sound, Dolby Atmos, and DTS:X
Ray tracing not supported on Xbox Series S
Single-player, Steam Achievements and Cloud
HDR available and Family Sharing listed
Early Bird Bonus presented separately on Steam
PS5 and Xbox include Hugh & Diana Outfit Set during active bonus window
Steam presents outfit bonus as Early Bird Bonus
Full game experience with no content removed
Shelter Variety Pack: extra outfits, weapon skin, BGM tracks
Diana gestures and artwork included
Hugh & Diana Outfit Set also shown on console stores

pragmataPreOrderBonus.title: Pragmata Pre-Order Bonus
pragmataPreOrderBonus.intro: The bonus content is centered on a matching costume pair for Hugh and Diana. Store pages describe the set as samurai- and ninja-inspired outfits from the Sengoku Period, and the wording is consistent across official listings: Neo Bushido for Hugh and Neo Kunoichi for Diana.

items.title: Bonus Items
items.description: The Early Bird Bonus contains two costumes: Hugh Outfit — Neo Bushido and Diana Outfit — Neo Kunoichi.
highlights: Two-character cosmetic set applies to both leads
Themed outfit reward with no gameplay impact
Listed consistently across all storefronts

items.title: Theme and Style
items.description: Official store pages describe the reward as samurai- and ninja-inspired costumes from the Sengoku Period.
highlights: Neo Bushido is Hugh's samurai-styled outfit
Neo Kunoichi is Diana's ninja-styled outfit
Designed as a paired visual set for both characters

items.title: Availability Window
items.description: After the launch moved forward, the digital pre-order bonus became an Early Bird Bonus. PlayStation lists it through April 23, 2026, while Steam shows equivalent cutoff times by region.
highlights: Steam cutoff: April 23, 2026 11:59 PM EDT
PlayStation Store: through April 23, 2026 11:59 PM
Xbox storefront shows outfit set included with pre-order editions

items.title: What Deluxe Adds Beyond the Bonus
items.description: The outfit set is not the whole Deluxe offer. Deluxe also includes the Shelter Variety Pack with extra outfits, a weapon skin, Shelter music tracks, Diana gestures, and artwork.
highlights: Standard focuses on the base game and active early purchase bonus
Deluxe adds the Shelter Variety Pack on top
Worth considering if you want cosmetic and Shelter-side extras

pragmataDeluxeEdition.title: Pragmata Standard vs Deluxe Edition
pragmataDeluxeEdition.intro: Pragmata is listed at $59.99 for Standard and $69.99 for Deluxe. The Deluxe upgrade centers on the Shelter Variety Pack: cosmetic outfits, a weapon skin, background music tracks, gestures, and artwork. Both editions include the full base game.

rows.standard: Neo Bushido + Neo Kunoichi outfits through Apr 23, 2026
rows.deluxe: Neo Bushido + Neo Kunoichi outfits through Apr 23, 2026

pragmataSystemRequirements.title: Pragmata PC System Requirements
pragmataSystemRequirements.intro: Pragmata requires Windows 11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 12, and 40 GB storage at both tiers. The recommended spec targets 1080p at 60 fps on the Balanced preset. Ray tracing requires a higher-end GPU listed below.

rows.minimum: Intel Core i5-8500 or AMD Ryzen 5 3500
rows.recommended: Intel Core i7-8700 or AMD Ryzen 5 5500

rows.minimum: NVIDIA GTX 1660 6 GB or AMD RX 5500 XT 8 GB
rows.recommended: NVIDIA RTX 2060 Super 8 GB or AMD RX 6600 8 GB
AMD RX 6700 XT 12 GB or NVIDIA RTX 3060 12 GB

pragmataWeaponsAndLoadouts.title: Pragmata Weapons, Units, and Loadout Options
pragmataWeaponsAndLoadouts.intro: Pragmata's combat is built around Hugh's guns and Diana's hacking nodes working together in real time. Multiple weapon roles, swappable tools, and hub-based loadout changes encourage distinct playstyles.

items.description: The one weapon that stays with Hugh regardless of ammo. All other special weapons break once depleted. Your Primary Unit is the fallback for every encounter.
Used to finish enemies at close range after a hack exposes weak points. Options include a shotgun for close-range bursts and the Charge Piercer rail gun for longer output.
Pins, slows, or knocks down enemies so you can safely complete hacks. The Stasis Net immobilizes targets; the Riot Blaster fires concussive blasts that knock enemies off their footing.
Pulls enemy attention away from Hugh or redirects pressure. A Decoy Generator sends enemies toward safe lanes or environmental traps, opening hacking windows without taking fire.
Decode lowers enemy defense, Multi-Hack spreads the effect to nearby targets, and Critical Down lets you knock down weakened enemies for a melee follow-up. Node choice shapes every fight.
Save points let you return to the Shelter to swap your loadout between encounters. Upgrades can unlock new abilities, add mod slots, and strengthen Hugh or Diana's toolkit.

pragmataEnemiesAndBosses.title: Pragmata Enemy Types and Boss Fight Patterns
pragmataEnemiesAndBosses.intro: Pragmata's enemies are designed to interrupt your combat rhythm. Shield bots force gunplay before hacking, missile bots add pressure during hacks, and colossal bosses turn the loop into a longer tactical exchange.

enemies.title: Standard Field Enemies
enemies.summary: Humanoid walkers, stomping mech-like bots, and drone-like flyers establish the normal combat baseline: hack to expose armor, then shoot weak points.
details: Sets up the normal flow of hacking to expose armor and then shooting weak points.
Most common enemies tied to weapon-role decisions and ammo management.
Multiple unit types active at once require short, targeted hacking.
enemies.lesson: Focus on target priority and keep hacks short when multiple units are active.

enemies.title: Shielded and Missile-Based Bots
enemies.summary: Red barrier bots block Diana's hacking until Hugh breaks the shield first. Missile bots add pressure during hacking and can be redirected back toward targets with a well-timed hack.
details: Red barriers force a switch from hacking back into gunplay before Diana can act.
Missile bots create pressure while you are drawing routes on the Hacking Panel.
A successful missile hack turns incoming fire into a counterattack.
enemies.lesson: Shield break or projectile control often comes before hacking in these encounters.

enemies.title: Advanced Disruptive Enemies
enemies.summary: Faster robots with broader hacking puzzle windows and hacking disruptor modules that interfere directly with Diana's ability to complete connections.
details: Move faster and use attack patterns that make multitarget fights more hectic.
Some are designed to interfere with hacking rather than simply absorb damage.
Node choice and crowd-control tools become more important than raw firepower here.
enemies.lesson: Bring control tools and choose hacking nodes before treating these fights like the demo's early battles.

enemies.title: Oddity-Class Enemies
enemies.summary: Strange designs including wheel-like enemies and a giant crawling bot with a baby-like face that opens to fire lasers. Unusual shapes make spacing and timing feel different.
details: Visually distinct enemies that push combat identity beyond standard drones and walkers.
Unusual body shapes change spacing and hit timing compared to normal bots.
Signal that enemy design keeps evolving rather than settling into one combat pattern.
enemies.lesson: Enemy silhouettes matter as much as their damage output in these fights.

enemies.title: Colossal Boss Encounters
enemies.summary: Massive bosses with area-of-effect attacks, multiple shield layers, and building-sized scale. One confirmed boss resembles a musclebound ant with a mantis-like face and a crushing neck attack.
details: Bosses use the same hack-and-shoot foundation but stretch it over a longer encounter.
Multiple shields force repeated transitions between shield breaking, hacking, and offense.
Hack progress is preserved when you back off to evade, changing the pacing of the fight.
enemies.lesson: Bosses reward patience, partial progress, and clean transitions between survival, hacking, and burst damage.

pragmataShelterAndUpgrades.title: Pragmata Shelter and Upgrades
pragmataShelterAndUpgrades.intro: PRAGMATA treats the Shelter as more than a pause room. It is the place where Hugh and Diana turn discoveries into permanent combat options, meet support bot Cabin, and launch back out toward unlocked parts of the station through the Tram and Escape Hatch network.

cards.name: 3D Printers
cards.description: Weapons and Hacking Nodes that you discover in the field become unlockable at the Shelter's 3D printers. Turns field discoveries into persistent upgrades covering both weapons and Hacking Nodes.

cards.name: Loadout Upgrades
cards.description: The Shelter is where Hugh and Diana strengthen their combat setup for tougher enemies deeper in the lunar facility. Use the hub to refine your next loadout between runs.

cards.name: Cabin
cards.description: Cabin is an independent support bot stationed in the Shelter and connected to the wider CABIN system. A friendly support presence inside the base, separate from the hostile systems controlling the station.

cards.name: Escape Hatches
cards.description: Escape Hatches are unlockable return points that tie exploration back to the Shelter. Unlock them as you advance through the station and they remain available as permanent fast routes.

cards.name: The Tram
cards.description: The Tram is the Shelter's travel link to the wider station, letting players move back out toward unlocked Escape Hatches. Makes backtracking and re-entry part of the exploration loop.

pragmataExplorationAndTraversal.title: Pragmata Exploration and Traversal
pragmataExplorationAndTraversal.intro: The lunar research station is designed as a layered space that opens through teamwork. Hugh supplies speed and movement, Diana opens blocked routes through hacking, and the Shelter network ties each push into a larger exploration loop.

steps.title: Use Hugh's Thrusters to Move Fast
steps.description: Hugh's suit lets him zip through the facility with thruster-assisted movement, making mobility a core part of exploration instead of a passive walk between fights.

steps.title: Let Diana Hack the Station
steps.description: Diana does not only hack enemies. She can also break into the station's security systems to unlock doors and clear blocked paths forward.

steps.title: Read the Station as a Multi-Layered Space
steps.description: The facility is structured to reward movement, path opening, and revisiting routes with better tools rather than simple corridor progression.

steps.title: Unlock Escape Hatches Along the Way
steps.description: Escape Hatches become key route anchors as you explore. They connect your outward progress back to the Shelter once they are unlocked.

steps.title: Return via the Tram
steps.description: From the Shelter, the Tram lets Hugh and Diana travel back toward unlocked Escape Hatches, turning exploration into a broader hub-and-spoke loop across the station.

steps.title: Stay Ready for Enemy Bots
steps.description: Even when the station feels empty, hostile bots remain part of the traversal rhythm, so movement, pathing, and combat readiness stay tightly linked.

pragmataDemoSecretsAndEndings.title: Pragmata Demo Secrets and Endings
pragmataDemoSecretsAndEndings.intro: The Sketchbook Demo is already useful as a combat sampler, but its bigger hook is replay value. PRAGMATA uses it to introduce traversal, showcase the hacking-and-shooting loop, and reward players who revisit it more than once.

faqs.question: What is the Pragmata Sketchbook Demo?
faqs.answer: PRAGMATA Sketchbook - DEMO is a free PS5 demo for the game. It is listed as a single-player action demo with offline play enabled, Remote Play support, PS5 Pro Enhanced support, and Game Help support.

faqs.question: Why should players replay the Pragmata demo?
faqs.answer: Xbox Wire explicitly says the Pragmata demo should be completed multiple times to uncover its secrets and different endings, making replaying part of the intended experience rather than optional busywork.

faqs.question: What does the Pragmata demo actually cover?
faqs.answer: The demo is designed to teach PRAGMATA's signature combat loop, where Diana's hacking and Hugh's shooting work together. It also lets players experience traversal through the lunar research station.

faqs.question: What platform and language details are available for the Pragmata demo?
faqs.answer: On PlayStation Store, the Pragmata demo is listed for PS5 with English and Japanese voice support, released February 5, 2026. The store page shows a 4.66 out of 5 average from 7,858 ratings.

pragmataTrailersAndNews.title: Pragmata Trailers and News
pragmataTrailersAndNews.intro: This timeline pulls together the major official beats that matter most for players landing on the game late. It places the important PRAGMATA trailers in order and shows what each one added to the public picture of the game.

entries.title: First Contact Trailer — June 2025
entries.description: Capcom reintroduced PRAGMATA with a clearer look at Hugh, Diana, the lunar station, and the game's two-in-one action-and-strategy pitch, while narrowing the release window to 2026.

entries.title: Pre-Order Trailer — December 2025
entries.description: Pre-orders opened and the game was publicly dated for April 24, 2026. This beat also pushed the broader public rollout around the Sketchbook demo.

entries.title: Main Trailer — March 5, 2026
entries.description: The newest PRAGMATA trailer added fresh enemy footage, new station areas, Shelter details, and Cabin, while moving the launch one week earlier from April 24 to April 17, 2026.

entries.title: Launch Date — April 17, 2026
entries.description: April 17, 2026 is now the live launch target presented across PRAGMATA's latest official promotional materials on all platforms.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Pragmata before launch

questions.question: What kind of game is Pragmata?
questions.answer: Pragmata is a single-player sci-fi action-adventure developed by Capcom. It features third-person shooting combined with a real-time grid-based hacking system controlled by the AI companion Diana.

questions.question: When does Pragmata release?
questions.answer: Pragmata releases on April 17, 2026 on Capcom's official site and PlayStation. Steam shows April 16, 2026 on its store page, reflecting a global same-day window.

questions.question: What platforms is Pragmata coming to?
questions.answer: Pragmata launches on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, Steam (PC), and Nintendo Switch 2. All four platforms receive the game in April 2026.

questions.question: Is there a free Pragmata demo available?
questions.answer: Yes. The Pragmata Sketchbook demo is free to download on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam. It includes live combat, hacking, and traversal sections from the lunar research station.

questions.question: Who are the main characters in Pragmata?
questions.answer: Hugh Williams is the playable protagonist, a member of the team sent to investigate the lost lunar facility. Diana is a Pragmata android who becomes his partner and handles the game's hacking system.

questions.question: What are the system requirements for Pragmata on PC?
questions.answer: Pragmata requires Windows 11 (64-bit) and approximately 40 GB of storage. Full minimum and recommended GPU and CPU specifications are listed on the official Steam store page.

cta.title: Ready to Explore PRAGMATA?
cta.description: Join the PRAGMATA community on Discord for guides, updates, and discussions about the hack-and-shoot sci-fi adventure!

character.title: Pragmata Characters
character.description: Character profiles for Hugh Williams, Diana, IDUS, and Cabin covering their roles, abilities, and story importance in Pragmata.
character.metaTitle: Pragmata Characters - Hugh, Diana, IDUS & Cabin
character.metaDescription: Complete Pragmata character profiles covering Hugh Williams, Diana the android, the IDUS rogue AI, and Cabin. Learn each character's role in combat and story.
character.whatIsDescription: Pragmata centers on Hugh Williams and Diana — an android — working together to escape a hostile lunar research station. IDUS is the rogue AI antagonist, and Cabin is their Shelter companion.

whySteps.step1Description: Hugh handles firearms while Diana controls the hacking panel in battle
whySteps.step2Description: Know each character's motivation and place in the larger narrative
whySteps.step3Description: Understand how the Hugh-Diana partnership drives both gameplay and story

version.title: Pragmata Platforms
version.description: Platform availability, edition differences, and version-specific features for Pragmata on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2.
version.metaTitle: Pragmata Platforms - PS5, Xbox, PC & Switch 2 Versions
version.metaDescription: Pragmata platform guide covering PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Steam PC, and Nintendo Switch 2 versions. Compare editions, pricing, and platform-exclusive features.
version.whatIsDescription: Pragmata releases on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC (Steam), and Nintendo Switch 2. Each platform has specific features and the Switch 2 Japan/Asia region launches one week later on April 24.

whySteps.step1Description: Compare PS5, Xbox, PC, and Switch 2 to find the best fit for your setup
whySteps.step2Description: Understand the difference between Standard and Deluxe editions
whySteps.step3Description: Review regional pricing and pre-order availability before launch

tech.title: Pragmata Tech
tech.description: PC system requirements, engine details, mod support, and technical specifications for Pragmata.
tech.metaDescription: Pragmata technical guide covering PC system requirements, RE Engine details, Denuvo DRM, mod support, and GeForce NOW cloud gaming compatibility.
tech.whatIsDescription: Technical details about Pragmata including minimum and recommended PC specs, the RE Engine powering the game, DRM information, and cloud gaming support via GeForce NOW.

whySteps.step1Description: Check your GPU and CPU against official minimum and recommended specifications
whySteps.step2Description: Learn how the RE Engine delivers Pragmata's visuals and performance targets
whySteps.step3Description: Find out early what modding support Pragmata will offer at launch

media.title: Pragmata Media
media.description: Official Pragmata trailers, wallpapers, soundtrack previews, and promotional artwork from Capcom.
media.metaDescription: Official Pragmata media including trailers, HD wallpapers, soundtrack previews, and promotional artwork. Watch all official Capcom videos and download assets.
media.whatIsDescription: Capcom has released multiple Pragmata trailers, HD wallpapers, and soundtrack previews. This section collects all official media assets in one place.

whySteps.step1Description: Catch up on every official Pragmata trailer from announcement to release
whySteps.step2Description: Get official HD wallpapers featuring Hugh, Diana, and the lunar research station
whySteps.step3Description: Listen to music previews and follow the official Pragmata YouTube channel
